Indian Diaspora-Fiji

# Indian Diaspora-Fiji

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

May 14, 2023

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

3

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

The Indian immigration to Fiji started in May 14, 1879 when Leonidas the first ship carrying Indian indentured laborers arrived. Around 400 Indian laborers would arrive to work on the sugarcane fields there, and soon would be an important part of Fiji.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZxjC!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F810268e4-ad55-4193-a3b5-6e57f8205a76_260x224.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZxjC!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F810268e4-ad55-4193-a3b5-6e57f8205a76_260x224.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!SkPz!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7b97331a-03c5-4b24-8393-211c7a1d70e6_292x200.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!SkPz!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7b97331a-03c5-4b24-8393-211c7a1d70e6_292x200.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TWra!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2361629c-9d48-4e48-b31b-f338ab1245e6_680x182.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TWra!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2361629c-9d48-4e48-b31b-f338ab1245e6_680x182.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!BEGf!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F593242ab-f6ba-47c8-a713-063860f25309_292x200.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!BEGf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F593242ab-f6ba-47c8-a713-063860f25309_292x200.png "Image")

Like Surinam, Guyana, Trinidad, most of the Indian laborers who were bought to Fiji were from eastern, central UP and Bihar. Called as Girimitiyas,they were bought by the British to work on the sugarcane fields here. While there were some Indians who arrived in batches earlier to work as labor, it was with the British colonization of Fiji, that the migration began to increase, or rather the forced migration.

Over the next 37 years, around 60000 Indians would arrive here, most of them from UP, Bihar, specifically the Bhojpuri area.The girmitiyas as they were called, were literal slaves. Many of them lived in apalling conditions, in dingy hovels. They were so called after the contract( girmit), which required them to work for 5 years in Fiji.

However with public outrage over the contract system, it was ended by the British in 1920. After the expiry of their contract many were given the option to go back to India or stay. Majority of them preferred to stay, being too poor to afford the return journey back home. They were either paid low wages, or in some cases none too. Many of them took to farming sugarcane, or cattle rearing, or setting up small businesses.

In a way this resulted in the emergence of the Fiji Indian, and with people from all castes working together, the caste system here was more or less done away with. It also resulted in development of Fiji Hindi, primarily a mix of Bhojpuri, Magahi and English. Soon you had the more educated Indians arriving in Fiji, that included doctors, teachers. The more prosperous farmers from Punjab and craftsmen from Gujarat too migrated here.

The Indian diaspora in Fiji usually called themselves as Fiji Indian or Indo-Fijian to distinguish themselves from the native Fijians. Typically the natives are defined as Fiji Islanders. While Badri Maharaj was appointed as the first Indian born Governor in 1916, it was a lawyer Manilal Maganlal who led the campaign for better treatment to the Indian diaspora there in Fiji. Sadhu Vashisht Muni led the agitation for better pay for the workers on Indian sugar plantations in 1920. Vishnu Deo, James Ramachander Rao and Parmanand Singh were the first Indians to be elected to Legislative Council.

The Hindu community here was split between two groups. One was the Arya Samajis, who advocated radical reforms, did not believe in rituals, another was the more conservative Sananthan Dharma. However most Fijian Hindus, adopted practices of both groups.

One of the more famous Hindu temples in Fiji is the Siva Subramania temple in the town of Nadi, built by the India Sanmarga Ikya Sangam, an association of South Indians settled in Fiji.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8Do2!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F966e17ec-0fbe-48cd-bca8-47565ea40686_220x143.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8Do2!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F966e17ec-0fbe-48cd-bca8-47565ea40686_220x143.jpeg "Image")

Between 1956-80, Indo-Fijians outnumbered the native Fijians, leading to fears of their domination. There was a heavy polarization between the two communities, that ultimately lead to the 1987 coup. The Indian community usually supported the National Federation Party founded by A.D.Patel or the Fiji Labor Party started by Mahendra Choudhary. However post 2000, both these parties have a lost considerable share of their votes.

Most of the Indians in Fiji are concentrated in the sugar belt, and urban areas on North, West coasts. Around 76% of the Indians here are Hindu, with the majority following the Sanathana Dharma group, while 3% follow the Arya Samaj. Good number of ISKCON followers too. Around 15% are Muslims, primarily Sunni, while the Indian Christians in Fiji mostly belong to the Methodist Church, and there is a small Sikh minority too. While the initial immigrants to Fiji were mostly from UP-Bihar, later fairly large numbers came from South, Gujarat.

Fijian Hindus celebrate Ram Navami, Holi, whle Diwali is a public holiday here. Along with temples, the Hindu community here built many schools, clinics.However there has been a large scale migration of Indians from Fiji post 2000, primarily due to the 2 military coups, by native Fijians( who were Christian) against the Hindu Fiji leaders in both cases.

Ever since Fiji became independent in 1970, tensions began to rise between the Indo-Fijians and native Fijians. One was the Indo-Fijians being economically well off, larger population. Another was that the native Fijians being Xtian primarily and the Indo-Fijians being Hindu.

In the case of the 1987 coup, it was the Indian born Timoci Bavadra, defeating Fiji's first PM, the native born Sir Kamises Mara, who had ruled since it's independence. Col Sitiveni Rabuka, led the coup, deposing Bavadra.

Again in 2000, another Indian born PM, Mahendra Choudhary was overthrown by Col George Speight. This was preceeded by racial violence in the late 90s against Indo-Fijians by native Fijians. Hindu owned shops, schools, temples were vandalized or destroyed.

The Methodist Church played an active role in Rabuka's 1987 Coup, and they have openly called for Fiji to be a Xtian State, and endorsed forceful conversion of Hindus. These incidents led many Hindus to migrate elsewhere.

Since 1987, around 100,000 Indo-Fijians have emigrated, following racial discriminations, attacks. Most of the professional, business class migrated, hitting Fiji's economy, especially it's sugar industry that was dependent on Indians. Most of the Indian community in Fiji migrated to Australia, New Zealand, Canada, and some to US after the 1987 coup, and the racial riots in the late 90s, as well as the 2000 coup. Many have done well in their adopted countries.

One of the well known Indo-Fijians is Rekha Sharma, whose family migrated from Fiji. Born in Vancouver, she was famous for her role as Tory Foster in the TV series Battleship Galactica.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cKDr!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fecec51c8-8c0d-445b-8c47-b5cbd6b4762d_201x250.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cKDr!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fecec51c8-8c0d-445b-8c47-b5cbd6b4762d_201x250.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Another well known Indo-Fijian is Nalini Singh, who moved to New Zealand as a child, and is the author a series of paranormal romance novels- Changelings,Guild Hunter, Rock Kiss.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eA1I!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd8abf676-749d-4653-a0e7-108e2e945d22_220x293.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eA1I!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd8abf676-749d-4653-a0e7-108e2e945d22_220x293.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

One of the top golfers in the world, Vijay Singh is of Fijian origin. He was No 1 in the rankings between 2004-05 and has won 2 PGA championships, One Masters title.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ekUM!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F443a6e97-9e00-4661-974a-58e16643a72f_275x183.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ekUM!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F443a6e97-9e00-4661-974a-58e16643a72f_275x183.jpeg "Image")
